auth_notes: Requires creating an 'Application Registry' entry in ServiceNow to generate a Client ID and Client Secret for OAuth 2.0. Basic Auth is supported but requires the 'rest_service' role assigned to the user.
rate_limits: Instance-specific; typically controlled by the 'REST API Rate Limiting' plugin which defaults to 1000 requests per minute per user.
gotchas: Pagination must be handled manually using 'sysparm_limit' and 'sysparm_offset' query parameters.
Reference fields return a sys_id by default; you must use 'sysparm_display_value=true' to get human-readable labels.
ServiceNow ACLs (Access Control Lists) can silently block access to specific fields even if the API call succeeds.
client_must_provide: OAuth Client ID and Client Secret
operations_available: Execute scripted REST APIs for custom logic
research_notes: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Use the 'Table API' for standard CRUD operations. For webhooks, ServiceNow requires a 'Business Rule' or 'Scripted REST API' to send an outbound HTTP request to the n8n webhook URL when records are created or updated.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Use the 'Table API' for standard CRUD operations. For webhooks, ServiceNow requires a 'Business Rule' or 'Scripted REST API' to send an outbound HTTP request to the n8n webhook URL when records are created or updated.
